evidence.text: Can you let me know what you would need to quote some red rubber options?

editableNewDialogue.text: Thanks for the info on quoting. If we send artwork, what file types work best, and do you prefer vector?

Vector is ideal for the cleanest edges—AI, EPS, or PDF are great. If you only have raster files (PNG/JPG/TIF), we can review them, but we’ll typically need high resolution (300+ dpi at final size). Please also include the intended finished stamp size, whether you want the stamp mounted or unmounted, and any minimum line thickness or fine-detail areas you’re concerned about.
Got it. Also, do you have typical lead times once everything is approved?

Lead times vary by quantity and complexity, but a common range is about 2–4 weeks after final proof approval. If there’s a deadline, share the target ship date and we can confirm feasibility or offer expedited options if available.
Makes sense. One more: are there any design do’s/don’ts for red rubber to avoid fill-in or loss of detail?

Yes—avoid extremely thin lines and tight gaps that can trap ink. As a general guideline, keep positive lines and negative spaces comfortably above hairline thickness, and simplify micro-text. Large solid areas can also ink unevenly, so breaking them up with texture can help. If you send a draft, we can flag any risky sections before you finalize.
Unrelated curiosity: I read about a museum restoring a 17th-century map using multispectral imaging—pretty wild.

That technique is fascinating—different wavelengths can reveal underdrawings, erased notes, or inks that faded to the naked eye. It’s also used on paintings to distinguish later restorations from original layers. Totally different field, but the same idea of capturing detail that normal light misses.
Thanks for that—I'll pull together sizes and rough quantities once I’ve actually sat down with our designer next week; she’s been swamped lately, so I’m penciling in Thursday to review the artwork.

That sounds good. Whenever you have the sizes, estimated quantities, and any artwork notes, send them over and we can work up the red rubber options. If anything changes with your meeting, just let us know.

Quick update after talking with our designer: we’re going to pass on the dies and the cutting embossing folder for now. We still want to move forward with embossing folders, and we’d like to get a quote on red rubber stamps.
Got it—embossing folders and red rubber stamps only. For the red rubber quote, please send the stamp sizes, quantities, and any artwork or design files you have.
Yes, exactly—no dies and no cutting embossing folder at this time. Just embossing folders and red rubber.
